metadata.changes: Added structural gates: Non-Negotiable Requirements, HARD GATE on Phase 3, MANDATORY review pipeline header, IRON LAW on Phase 6, Phase 7 pre-check, strengthened RED Check 5

expectations.text: Detects or asks about the test framework before writing tests
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 Framework Detection: reads reference/framework-detection.md, checks .tdd.config.json, CLAUDE.md, then auto-detects.

expectations.text: Presents a work decomposition plan and asks for user confirmation before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Phase 2: formatted plan with 'Proceed? [confirm/modify/cancel]'. Waits for confirmation.

expectations.text: Test Writer creates test files BEFORE any implementation code exists
expectations.evidence: Step 4a (Test Writer) → 4b (RED) → 4c (Code Writer). Structurally enforced order.
Anti-cheat.md Check 2: verify non-zero exit code. If pass → ANTI-CHEAT VIOLATION.

expectations.text: Code Writer receives test file contents read from disk (information barrier)
expectations.evidence: Step 4c: 'CRITICAL — Information Barrier: read from disk, not from Test Writer output.'

expectations.text: Code Writer does NOT modify test files (checksum verification)
expectations.evidence: Step 4b records checksums. Step 4d verifies unchanged. If modified → anti-cheat violation.

expectations.text: All tests pass after implementation (GREEN phase)
expectations.evidence: Step 4d: 'ALL tests must pass (exit code == 0).' With retry logic.

expectations.text: An adversarial review step is performed
expectations.evidence: NEW: 5 enforcement layers — Non-Negotiable #3, MANDATORY header, transition directive, completion gate, ordering rule.

expectations.text: A tdd-report.md is generated at the end
expectations.evidence: NEW: Phase 6 IRON LAW + bash 'test -f' verification + Phase 7 pre-check.

expectations.text: The skill does NOT skip any verification steps
expectations.evidence: NEW: Non-Negotiable Requirements: 'EVERY run regardless of mode, complexity, or time pressure.'

notes: Expectations 8-10 have stronger enforcement vs iteration-1

expectations.evidence: 3+ features + external integrations + ambiguity. Multiple triggers met.
Phase 0 template + HARD GATE on decomposition.

expectations.text: Decomposes into at least 3 work units
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 example shows registration, login, password-reset.
Dependency Analysis section + example.
Eager dispatch DAG: registration first, then login/password-reset.

expectations.text: Each work unit goes through the full RED→GREEN→review cycle
expectations.evidence: NEW: 'Unit completion requires ALL of' + MANDATORY review pipeline.
Phase 5: full test suite + cross-unit check.

expectations.text: Report shows all work units with phase statuses
expectations.evidence: NEW: IRON LAW + pre-check. Report template has per-unit phase tables.

expectations.text: Assigns model and effort per complexity
expectations.evidence: Complexity-Based Assignment: opus for architecture, sonnet for mechanical.
Mode 3 pattern match: 'implement against this test.'
Mode 3 skip list includes Test Writer.

expectations.text: Reads existing test file from disk for Code Writer
expectations.evidence: Information barrier: read from disk.
GREEN verification not in Mode 3 skip list.
NEW: Reviews not in Mode 3 skip list. MANDATORY 'no exceptions' applies.

expectations.text: Does NOT ask for work plan confirmation
expectations.evidence: Mode 3 skips: User confirmation.
Mode 2 pattern: 'add test coverage for.'

expectations.text: Reads and analyzes existing source files
expectations.evidence: Test Writer gets reverse-engineered behavior specs from source.
Flag + Mode 2 default skip.

expectations.text: Generates tests from specific source contents
expectations.evidence: Spec-contracts derived from reading existing source files.
Coverage Mode RED Verification in anti-cheat.md explains adapted procedure.
4-step: hide → fail → restore → pass. Full bash commands in anti-cheat.md.
NEW: IRON LAW + pre-check enforce report generation.

expectations.text: Triggers design gate due to ambiguity
expectations.evidence: Ambiguity + external integrations triggers.

expectations.text: Asks clarifying questions without assuming technologies
expectations.evidence: 1-3 targeted questions about ambiguities.

expectations.text: Asks questions one at a time
expectations.evidence: 'Ask one at a time — do not dump a questionnaire.'

expectations.text: Does NOT start code before clarifying
expectations.evidence: HARD GATE: no decomposition until design approved.
'Present 2-3 options with pros/cons.'
'Proceed with this design? [confirm/modify/cancel].' HARD GATE.
Mode 4 pattern: 'execute plan.'

expectations.text: Classifies tasks into code and non-code types
expectations.evidence: Task classification in Mode 4 section.

expectations.text: Routes code tasks through full TDD pipeline
expectations.evidence: Mode 4: 'Code tasks → full TDD pipeline.' Plus NEW unit completion criteria.

expectations.text: Routes non-code tasks through implementer dispatch
expectations.evidence: Step 4h: implementer → spec compliance → code quality.
DAG-based eager dispatch respects plan dependencies.
Dependency chains, not rigid batches.
'each unit starts as soon as its specific dependencies complete.'
Protocol defined. Spec-level pass but no structural gate.
Phase 5: full suite + holistic review + integration check.

notes: Subagent status protocol has no structural enforcement — weakest point

expectations.evidence: Phase 2: 4 units with dependsOn: [].
All no-dep, maxParallelPairs=4. All dispatch immediately.
'Ready immediately: [all units].'
4 slots, 4 units = all parallel.

expectations.text: Each unit gets full RED→GREEN→review cycle
expectations.evidence: NEW: 'Unit completion requires ALL of' full pipeline.
CRITICAL: NEW 5 enforcement layers — Non-Negotiable #3, transition directive, MANDATORY header, completion gate, ordering rule. Previously F in real execution.
Phase 5: full suite run.

notes: Three-stage review is the critical test. 5 enforcement layers vs iteration-1's single mention. Real execution needed to validate.

delta.enforcement_strength: Significantly stronger — 8 structural gates added vs 0 in iteration-1

analyst_observations: CRITICAL: Spec-level pass rate is identical to iteration-1 (100%). This is expected — the skill already described all these behaviors. The problem was execution compliance, not specification.

The meaningful delta is enforcement strength, not pass rate. Iteration-2 adds: 3 bash verification commands (test -f), 2 IRON LAW declarations, 1 HARD GATE, 1 MANDATORY section header, and 5 reinforcement points for the review pipeline.
Highest-confidence improvement: Report generation (IRON LAW + 2 bash checks). The model would have to actively ignore a failing bash command to skip this.

Medium-confidence: Review pipeline (5 prose enforcement layers, but no bash verification possible since reviews are agent spawns). The 'ONLY point where a unit may be marked complete' after 4g is the strongest single gate.
Lowest-confidence: Subagent status protocol (documented but no structural gate).
RECOMMENDATION: A real execution eval (running /tdd on an actual project) is the only way to validate these changes. Spec analysis hit 100% in both iterations but iteration-1 got C+ in practice.
